atoms.semantic_claim: The reth-errors crate exposes an async-context feature that forwards to reth-execution-errors/async-context so async-context validation variants are available through the error re-export chain.

destinations.evidence: The destination reth-errors manifest contains the same async-context feature forwarding.
The forwarded dependency feature exists and links the errors crate to alloy-evm async-context variants.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Compile reth-errors with --features async-context and use re-exported BlockExecutionError/BlockValidationError paths that depend on async-context-gated variants.
atoms.notes: High risk because this gates async-context error propagation, but the file-level manifest atom is exact in the destination.

summary: The async-context feature forwarding in crates/errors/Cargo.toml is present exactly in the v2.2.0 port.
notes: Fresh child audit found exact destination feature plumbing and matching downstream dependency forwarding through reth-execution-errors to alloy-evm.
